data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Warns of Forex Market Intervention Ahead of BOJ Decision
data.description:
Japan's Finance Minister Satuki Katayama has issued a warning to speculators, stating that authorities are prepared to respond to forex market fluctuations at any time. According to Jin10, this announcement comes just hours before the Bank of Japan is set to reveal its policy decision. Katayama emphasized the readiness to take decisive action if necessary, and when asked about the government's vigilance during Japan's upcoming Golden Week holiday, she confirmed their preparedness for round-the-clock intervention.

The Bank of Japan is expected to announce its latest interest rate decision shortly, with the market widely anticipating no change. The voting outcome will be closely watched as the market assesses the possibility of a rate hike in June. Governor Kazuo Ueda's remarks at the post-decision press conference will also be scrutinized.

Pricing in the overnight swap market indicates a less than 3% probability of a rate hike on Tuesday, while the likelihood of a June rate increase has risen to 65%. The USD/JPY is currently trading around 159.5, near the level where authorities intervened in the forex market in 2024.

data.title: Elon Musk's Lawsuit Against OpenAI Commences in California Court
data.description:
Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI began on April 27 in the federal court in Oakland, California, with jury selection underway. According to Odaily, opening statements are expected to start on Tuesday. Prior to the trial, both parties engaged in a public dispute on the X platform, with OpenAI dismissing the lawsuit as baseless, while Musk criticized Sam Altman, referring to him as 'Scam Altman.'

The case centers on the controversy surrounding OpenAI's transition to a for-profit model. Musk accuses OpenAI of violating its original non-profit commitment and is seeking up to $134 billion in damages. He also demands the reversal of the for-profit transition and the removal of relevant executives. The liability phase of the trial is anticipated to continue until mid-May.

data.title: Polymarket received 122 million visits in Q1, ranking first among crypto application websites.
data.description: According to data from Similarweb, in Q1 of 2026, Polymarket ranked first among crypto application websites with 122 million visits, surpassing Robinhood (118 million visits). Coinbase ranked third (78.8 million visits), Kalshi sixth (34.8 million visits), Kraken eighth (22 million visits), Hyperliquid ninth (12.8 million visits), Pump.fun eleventh (8.2 million visits), and Uniswap twelfth (5 million visits).

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Signals Potential Intervention Amid Yen Volatility
data.description: On April 28, Jin10 reported that the Commonwealth Bank of Australia's global economics and market research team highlighted a complex situation for the USD/JPY exchange rate this week. If the ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions continue to drive oil prices higher, the USD/JPY is expected to rise slightly. Additionally, the ambiguous stance of Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda regarding a potential interest rate hike at the June meeting poses a risk. However, a rapid increase in the currency pair might prompt Japan's Ministry of Finance to intervene in the foreign exchange market to support the yen. Japan's Finance Minister Satuki Katayama previously stated that recent forex fluctuations are speculative, and authorities will take decisive action to intervene in the forex market if necessary.

data.title: Anta Plans to Re-enter Indian Market with New Store Launch
data.description: Chinese sportswear company Anta is set to re-enter the Indian market next month by opening a store in Gurugram, a suburb of New Delhi. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic move to tap into the world's most populous market. Anta's local partner confirmed the plans, marking a significant step for the brand as it seeks to expand its global footprint. The store launch is part of Anta's broader strategy to increase its presence in international markets, leveraging India's growing consumer base and demand for sportswear. This move follows previous attempts by the company to establish itself in India, reflecting its commitment to capturing market share in the region.

data.title: Crypto-Linked U.S. Stocks Decline as CRCL Leads Losses
data.description: Crypto-linked U.S. stocks experienced a downturn, with CRCL leading the decline by over 4.22%. According to NS3.AI, the broader market showed mixed results, as the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell by 0.13%, while the Nasdaq Composite Index rose by 0.2% and the S&P 500 increased by 0.12%.

data.title: South Korean Stocks Hit Record High as KOSPI Rises
data.description: South Korean stocks have reached a new record high, with the KOSPI index climbing 1% intraday to 6,681.43. According to NS3.AI, this movement marks a new all-time high for the index, reflecting positive investor sentiment in the market.

data.title: Gold and crude oil rose slightly, while the volatility indices for BTC and ETH fell.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,693.08 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.24%. Silver prices rose to $75.876 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.53%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 41.67, a daily decrease of 0.10%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 59.90, a daily decrease of 2.12%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate remained unchanged at 6.82571. The USD/JPY exchange rate remained unchanged at 159.407. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 fell 0.58% to 5,872.05 points; the UK FTSE 100 rose 0.12% to 10,333.87 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.16% to 24,141.31 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 0.20% to $99.65 per barrel, and Brent crude oil rose 0.28% to $108.50 per barrel. Gate allows users to trade traditional financial market products directly on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: The trial of Musk v. OpenAI has begun, with the two sides escalating their public exchanges before the trial.
data.description: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI began on April 27th in the U.S. District Court in Oakland, California, with jury selection commenced. Opening statements are expected to begin on Tuesday. Prior to the trial, the two sides escalated their public exchanges on the X platform, with OpenAI claiming the lawsuit is baseless, while Musk repeatedly attacked Sam Altman, calling him "Scam Altman." The core of the case revolves around the controversy surrounding OpenAI's for-profit transformation. Musk accuses OpenAI of violating its founding non-profit commitment and is seeking up to $134 billion in damages, while also demanding the reversal of the for-profit transformation and the removal of relevant executives. The liability determination phase is expected to continue until mid-May.

data.title: South Korean exchange Coinone has sued the Financial Intelligence Service of Korea, seeking to halt regulatory penalties.
data.description: South Korean cryptocurrency exchange Coinone has filed a lawsuit against the Financial Intelligence Service (FIU) of Korea and applied to the Seoul Administrative Court for a stay of execution, attempting to suspend the sanctions before they take effect on April 29. The core of the sanctions is to restrict new users from depositing and withdrawing cryptocurrency assets to external exchanges. Previously, the FIU imposed a three-month partial business suspension and a fine of 5.2 billion won on Coinone for violating the Specific Financial Information Act. (Naver)

data.title: Bridge Biotherapeutics Rebrands as Parataxis Korea, Shifts Focus to Digital Assets
data.description: Bridge Biotherapeutics has rebranded itself as Parataxis Korea, marking a strategic pivot towards a digital asset treasury business. According to NS3.AI, this change comes in the wake of a sale of management rights and a comprehensive business restructuring. The decision follows the unsuccessful development of the company's primary biotech pipeline, prompting a shift in business strategy.

data.title: Crypto Council CEO Highlights Urgency for Clarity Act Progress in U.S. Senate
data.description: Ji Kim, CEO of the Crypto Council for Innovation, emphasized the limited time available for the U.S. Senate to advance the Clarity Act, with only about 9 to 10 working weeks remaining before the August congressional recess. According to NS3.AI, the bill must still pass through the Senate Banking Committee, where disagreements over stablecoin yield and rewards continue to pose significant challenges.

data.title: Polymarket to Distribute $1 Million in Liquidity Rewards During CLOBv2 Migration
data.description: Polymarket has announced plans to distribute $1 million in liquidity rewards as part of its CLOBv2 migration. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that $500,000 of these rewards will be allocated within the first two hours following the migration.

data.title: CATL to Raise HK$39.19 Billion Through H-Share Placement
data.description: CATL has announced plans to raise approximately HK$39.19 billion by issuing 62,385,000 new H-shares at a price of HK$628.20 each. According to NS3.AI, the company expects net proceeds of around HK$39.11 billion after accounting for associated costs. CATL intends to allocate these funds towards global new energy projects, zero-carbon business initiatives, research and development, working capital, and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Polymarket has launched a new prediction event: "Jimmy Kimmel will be fired/resign before May 31st".
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, a new prediction event on Polymarket, "Jimmy Kimmel will be fired/resign before May 31st," currently has a 9% probability of being "yes." The event's contract rules state that if Jimmy Kimmel ceases hosting his talk show, "Jimmy Kimmel Live!", between the contract's creation date and 11:59 PM ET on May 31, 2026, the market result is "yes"; otherwise, it is "no." If Jimmy Kimmel resigns/is fired before the event's end date, the market result will immediately be "yes," regardless of when the resignation/firing takes effect; suspension, administrative leave, or other temporary measures do not qualify. The market's primary source of results is official information from ABC or Disney, but it also considers broad consensus from other reliable reports. US President Trump has called on ABC to fire late-night talk show host Jimmy Kimmel after Kimmel made jokes about First Lady Melania Trump on his show. Melania Trump has also suggested that Jimmy Kimmel should lose his job for his comments. Trump posted that "Jimmy Kimmel should be fired immediately from Disney and ABC," and accused the joke of being a "despicable act of inciting violence." Odaily Seer, the predictive channel, continues to focus on the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: A whale, aiming for "10 major targets," has incurred a floating loss of $178,000 on its long position of 397.7 BTC.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa), as BTC briefly dipped below $77,000, the long position of 397,718 BTC previously opened by the whale "First Set 10 Big Targets" is currently showing a floating loss of approximately $178,000, with the loss widening to $488,000 at one point during the trading session. Furthermore, a previous long order on Binance at $77,686.50, containing 2,759.11 BTC and valued at approximately $214 million, is speculated by the community to belong to this whale. If true, this position currently shows a floating loss of approximately $1,237,000.

data.title: South Korean stocks hit a new record high
data.description: On April 28, the Seoul Composite Index in South Korea rose by 1% to 6,681.43 points, setting a new record high.

data.title: OpenAI Faces Financial Challenges Despite High User Numbers
data.description: OpenAI is experiencing financial difficulties as its user base grows, according to PANews. Despite having a large number of users, 95% of them do not pay for the service, leading to losses even with the $200 Pro subscription. The company is facing a situation where increased user numbers are resulting in greater financial losses.

data.title: CATL Announces Share Placement Agreement in Hong Kong
data.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited (CATL) has announced a share placement agreement on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. According to Odaily, the company has entered into an agreement with placement agents to facilitate the subscription of 62,385,000 shares by at least six independent third-party subscribers. The placement price is set at HKD 628.20 per share. The total proceeds from the placement are approximately HKD 39.19 billion, with net proceeds estimated at HKD 39.11 billion after deducting commissions and related expenses. The funds raised will be allocated to global new energy projects, zero-carbon business development, research and development, and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Trump Reportedly Dissatisfied with Iran's Proposal on Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has reportedly expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's latest proposal concerning the reopening of the Hormuz Strait and ending the ongoing conflict. According to Odaily, sources revealed this information to The New York Times.
